exercises.name: Machine Seated Single Arm Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
1.Sit upright at a shoulder press machine, with one hand holding the inside handle at shoulder height, palm facing in and elbow bent.

2.Press the handle overhead, extending your arm fully.

3.Lower the handle returning to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Single Arm Supinated Grip Row
exercises.description:
1.Sit at a reverse position in a machine, chest against the backrest. One hand holding the horizontal position handle, shoulder parallel to the hand.

2.Pull the handle into you and try to squeeze your shoulder blades together in the back Pause.

3.Slowly return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Single Leg Calf Raise
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, put the ball of your foot on the step. 

2.Place your lower thigh under the lever pad, which will need to be adjusted according to the height of your thigh. Now place your hands on top of the lever pad in order to prevent it from slipping forward. 

3.In order to get full range of motion, ensure that you point your toe as far as you can. And on the opposite direction, you let your heel drop down as low as it will go.

4.Lift the lever slightly by pushing your heel up and release the safety bar. This will be your starting position.

5.Slowly lower your heel by bending at the ankle until the calf is fully stretched. Inhale as you perform this movement.

6.Raise the heel by extending the ankle as high as possible as you contract the calf and breathe out. Hold the top contraction for a second.

6. When you’ve completed that side, repeat on the other.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Single Leg Curl
exercises.description:
1.Adjust the machine lever to fit your height and sit on the machine with your back against the back support pad. Leg fully straight in front of you, back of the lower leg on top of the padded lever. Grasp the side handles on the machine as you point your toes straight.

2.Curl your leg downward as far as possible.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Machine Seated Supinated Grip Row
exercises.description:
1.Sit at a reverse position in a machine, chest against the backrest. Both hands holding the horizontal position handles in an underhand position, palms facing up.

2.Pull the handles into you and try to squeeze your shoulder blades together in the back Pause.

3.Slowly return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Single Leg Extension
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, you’ll be working your quadriceps.

2.Ensure that the pad is slightly above the ankle and that your thigh is well supported.

3.Also, make sure the weight doesn’t rest in between each repetition.

4.Extend your knee fully, raising your leg straight out. 

5.Slowly return to starting position.

exercises.name: Machine Standing Calf Raise
exercises.description: Stand straight at a standing calf raise machine with your shoulders under the pads and your heels hanging off the foot step. Begin by raising up on your toes as high as you can go, pause, and then return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Machine Standing Single Leg Curl
exercises.description:
1) Stand straight at a standing leg curl machine with the pad behind one of your ankles. 

2) Begin by curling your leg up as far as you can, pause, and return to starting position. 

3) After you finish the desired number of reps, switch legs.

exercises.name: Machine Sumo Leg Press
exercises.description:
1. Sit on a leg press machine, and place your feet against the pad wider than shoulder-width apart. Make sure your knees are bent at the start of the exercise.

2. Slowly push against the pad using your heels until your knees are almost straight. Do not lock your knees straight at the peak of this exercise. Then move back into the start position.

3. Repeat for each rep.

exercises.name: Machine Tricep Pushdowns
exercises.description:
1. Start seated on the tricep pushdown machine, with your hands on the handles. 

2. Slowly raise the weight so that your forearms come up to touch your biceps, while your elbows move backwards. 

3. Push the handles all the way down until your elbows are straight. Repeat.

exercises.name: Machine Weighted Back Extensions
exercises.description: Lie face down on the hamstring curl machine. Place your ankles under the pad. Curl the pad toward your glutes by bending your knees, then slowly lower back down.

exercises.name: Marching Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Perform a glute bridge while alternating marching your legs in the air one at a time.

exercises.name: Marching On The Spot
exercises.description: March in place, focusing on controlled movements and alternating legs while engaging the core.

exercises.name: Medicinal Ball Alternating Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold a medicine ball in front of you. 

Step forward into a lunge with one leg, then return to standing and alternate with the other leg, while keeping the ball in front of you.

exercises.name: Medicinal Ball Alternating Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold a medicine ball at chest height. 

Step backward into a reverse lunge, alternating legs with each repetition, ensuring a deep lunge and proper form.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Alternating Lunge And Twist
exercises.alternateName: med ball reverse lunge with rotation
exercises.description: Perform a lunge while holding a medicine ball. As you lunge, twist your torso over the front leg, alternating sides with each lunge and twist.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Alternating Overhead Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold a medicine ball overhead. 

Step into a forward lunge, alternating legs, while keeping the ball overhead for stability and core engagement.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Alternating Overhead Reverse Lunge
exercises.description: Hold a medicine ball overhead. Step back into a reverse lunge, alternating legs, keeping the ball overhead and engaging the core throughout.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Alternating Split Jumps
exercises.description:
Hold a medicine ball close to your chest. Start in a split stance with one foot forward and one back. 

Jump explosively, switching your legs in mid-air, and land softly in a split stance on the opposite side. 

Keep the ball steady as you perform the jumps.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Calf Raises
exercises.description: Hold a medicine ball at your chest. Perform calf raises by lifting your heels off the ground and lowering slowly.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Flutter Kicks
exercises.alternateName: med ball swimmer kicks, scissor kicks
exercises.description: Lie on your back with a medicine ball on your chest. Perform flutter kicks, keeping your core engaged.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Half Kneel Wood Chop
exercises.description:
Kneel on one knee with a medicine ball in both hands. 

Twist your torso as you bring the ball from a high position to a low one, focusing on the obliques.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Half Kneeling Alternating Slams
exercises.description:
Kneel with one knee on the ground and the other foot planted in front for stability. Hold a medicine ball with both hands. 

Alternate slamming the ball to the ground on either side of your body, using a full range of motion from overhead to the floor. 

Focus on controlled, explosive power with each slam.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Hollow Hold Press
exercises.alternateName: Medicine Ball banana Hold chest Press
exercises.description:
Lie on your back holding a medicine ball close to your chest. Lift your shoulders and legs off the floor, keeping your lower back pressed into the ground to form a hollow position. 

While maintaining core tension, press the medicine ball straight up over your chest, then lower it back down with control. 

Continue pressing while holding the hollow position throughout the exercise.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Knee Drives
exercises.description: Hold a medicine ball overhead. Drive one knee up to your chest while pulling the ball down. Alternate legs.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Kneeling Slam
exercises.description:
In a kneeling position, hold a medicine ball above your head. 

Slam it to the ground with force, using your core and upper body, then catch and repeat.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Leg Drops
exercises.description: Hold a medicine ball with your feet. Lower your legs toward the floor, then raise them back up while engaging your core.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Low Plank Roll Outs
exercises.description: From a low plank, place forearms on a medicine ball. Roll the ball forward, then pull it back while engaging your core.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Lunge
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding the ball with both hands, just under the chin and against your chest.

2.Take a step forward, dropping your back knee toward the floor, keeping the ball against your chest.

3.Push off your front foot returning to starting position.

4.Repeat and alternate.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Lunge Twist to Overhead Press
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a medicine ball at chest level. Step one foot back into a reverse lunge and, as you lower down, rotate your torso toward your front leg while keeping the ball in front of your chest. 

As you push back up to standing, drive through your front heel and simultaneously press the medicine ball overhead. 

Lower the ball back to your chest and repeat, alternating legs each rep.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Mountain Climbers
exercises.description: Place hands on a medicine ball. Perform mountain climbers by alternately driving knees toward your chest.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Oblique Slam
exercises.alternateName: med ball side to side slams
exercises.description:
Hold a medicine ball with both hands. 

Slam the ball down to one side of your body while engaging your obliques, then catch and repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Oblique Slams Kneeling
exercises.description:
Start in a tall kneeling position with your knees on the floor and your core braced. 

Hold a medicine ball with both hands. Twist your torso to one side, raising the ball overhead, then powerfully slam the ball to the ground on the same side. 

Catch the ball, reset, and repeat on the opposite side. Maintain a straight back throughout.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Over Head Backward Slam
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, medicine ball on the floor in front of you. Hinge at your hips and slightly bend your knees to grab the ball. 

Lift the ball overhead, fully extending your arms, then engage your core and explosively slam the ball backward toward the floor behind you. 

Retrieve the ball, return to the starting position, and repeat continuously in a controlled, powerful rhythm.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Over Shoulder Toss
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, medicine ball on the floor in front of you. 

Hinge at your hips and slightly bend your knees to grab the ball. Using your legs, hips, and core, explosively toss the ball over one shoulder behind you. 

Retrieve the ball, return to the starting position, and repeat the toss on the opposite shoulder. Continue alternating sides with controlled, powerful movements.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Overhead Lateral Bounds
exercises.description:
Hold a medicine ball overhead with arms extended. Jump laterally to one side, landing on one foot, and bring the opposite foot down for balance. 

Immediately jump to the other side. Keep the ball overhead and your movements controlled to engage your core and stabilizers.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Overhead Lunge
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding a ball in both hands, arms up, extended and straight.

2.Keeping your arms straight upwards, step forward with your one foot in a lunge motion and bend at both knees till they make a 90-degree angle.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat and alternate.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Overhead Side Bend
exercises.description:
Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a medicine ball overhead with your arms fully extended. 

Keep your core engaged and your arms straight as you slowly bend your torso to one side, feeling a stretch along your opposite oblique. 

Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side, alternating in a controlled motion.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Pass Crunches
exercises.alternateName: Med ball pass to legs core
exercises.description: Hold a medicine ball at your chest. Perform crunches by lifting your shoulders off the ground and engaging your core.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Pass Extended Crunches
exercises.description: Hold a medicine ball overhead. Perform crunches, keeping the ball extended behind you for added resistance.
